stories.title: Misiorowski Throws Fastest Pitches in MLB History for a Starter — 103.6 MPH vs. Yankees

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Jacob Misiorowski just threw the fastest pitches ever recorded by a starting pitcher in MLB history. 103.6 mph. Seven pitches at 103.2+ mph — all in one game vs the Yankees. 11 K, 6 scoreless innings. That is not human. #Brewers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: For context: the fastest pitch ever by a starter was 103.2 mph (Jordan Hicks, 2022). Misiorowski just threw SEVEN pitches faster than that in a single game. He also threw 43 pitches at 100+ mph — 3rd most ever in one game. #Brewers #MLB

article.headline: Misiorowski Throws Fastest Pitches in MLB History for a Starter — 103.6 MPH in Dominant Gem vs. Yankees

stories.title: Aaron Judge at 383 Career HRs — On Pace for Historic 2026 Season, 400 Club in Sight

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: Aaron Judge has 383 career home runs and 15 already this season. At his current pace, he hits No. 400 sometime this summer — and a 5th career 50-HR season (never been done) is within reach. Oh, and Goldschmidt is also chasing 400 this year. Yankees history incoming. #Yankees #MLB

article.headline: Aaron Judge at 383 Career Home Runs — The 400 Club Is a 2026 Achievement Waiting to Happen

stories.title: Cubs Win Streak Ends — Rangers Shut Out Chicago 6-0, But Wrigley Wonders Continue

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Rangers held the Cubs scoreless 6-0 on Saturday, ending Chicago's 2nd 10-game win streak of 2026. But the Cubs are still 27-13 and leading the NL Central. The Wrigley streak? Still alive. Hard to be too upset about that. #Cubs #MLB

stories.title: Braves 26-12 — NL East Lead Swells to 8.5 Games, Division May Already Be Over

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The Atlanta Braves are 26-12 with an 8.5-game lead in the NL East, and nobody else in the division is above .500. Run differential: +79. FanGraphs gives them a 95% shot at the postseason. The NL East race may already be over. #Braves #MLB

stories.title: Blue Jays Rout Angels 14-1 — Valenzuela's Four-Hit Game, Near Cycle

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: The Toronto Blue Jays dropped 14 runs on the Angels on Saturday. Luis Valenzuela went 4-for-5 with 4 RBI in his first career four-hit game — and came within a triple of the cycle. That's a day at the ballpark. #BlueJays #MLB

stories.title: Weekend MLB Roundup — Streaks, Records, and Sunday Baseball

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text: What a week of baseball: Misiorowski breaks the velocity record for starters (103.6 mph). Judge marches toward 400 career HRs. Cubs hit two 10-game win streaks. Braves run away with the NL East. Blue Jays drop 14 runs on LA. See you Monday. #MLB
